prevalence.source: WHO/CDC zoster epidemiology context; Indian cohort summaries

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; post-herpetic neuralgia significant in older adults

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Typical dermatomal vesicular rash with neuropathic pain; PCR when atypical or disseminated

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of episodes annually (age-dependent)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; referral for ocular or disseminated disease
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acyclovir/valacyclovir within 72 hours, analgesia, neuropathic pain management; vaccination mainly private
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited adult zoster vaccine access and delayed presentation
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Antivirals are affordable and widely available
